# AIRelays

AIRelays is a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP server with provider-scoped runtimes.

- The default runtime uses an AIRelays-owned ChatGPT subscription login.
- The local experimental branch also enables a Claude runtime that uses the local `claude` CLI for explicit `claude:*` text models.
- AIRelays protects `/v1/*` and `/no-tools/v1/*` with its own relay bearer token by default.
- Open local relay mode applies to all enabled providers.

## First Run

Install:

```bash
python -m pip install airelays
```

OpenAI runtime:

```bash
airelays init
airelays login
airelays doctor
airelays serve --port 8080
```

Claude experimental runtime:

```bash
airelays init
claude auth login --claudeai
airelays serve --port 8080
```

Claude headless:

```bash
airelays init
claude setup-token
export CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN='YOUR_CLAUDE_TOKEN'
airelays serve --port 8080
```

## Runtime Shape

OpenAI runtime:

- `GET /v1/models`
- `POST /v1/responses`
- `POST /v1/chat/completions`
- `POST /v1/completions`
- local files
- local conversations
- `GET /v1/subscription/status`
- successful OpenAI upstream model-list responses are cached in memory for `models_cache_ttl_seconds` seconds

Claude experimental runtime:

- explicit `claude:*` model ids
- `POST /v1/chat/completions`
- `POST /v1/completions`
- model ids starting with `claude:` route to this runtime when it is enabled
- other model ids route to the OpenAI runtime when it is enabled
- stateless only
- no `/v1/responses`
- no files, images, audio, tools, or structured outputs

## Security

- relay bearer auth required by default
- open local relay mode applies to all enabled providers, including Claude experimental mode
- Claude experimental mode requires loopback binding
- request and response contents can be written to local AIRelays log files; bearer tokens are redacted
- repeated bad tokens trigger a temporary IP block

## Diagnostics

- `airelays status` reports local relay, token, OpenAI login, and provider readiness state.
- `airelays doctor` runs local setup checks, validates the relay token state, checks OpenAI login readiness, probes upstream `/models`, and sends a tiny `/responses` smoke request when the OpenAI runtime is enabled and logged in.
- `airelays doctor --skip-response` skips the generation smoke request.
